evals.prompt: I need a quote for a deep clean of a 4-bedroom house, 2200 sqft. They also want the fridge and oven cleaned inside, plus 8 windows. They're interested in biweekly recurring service.
evals.expected_output: A formatted quote showing deep clean base price + add-ons (fridge $35, oven $40, windows 8×$5=$40), subtotal, 10% biweekly discount, and final total. Estimated duration ~5 hours.
expectations: The CLI quote command is used with --service-type deep --sqft 2200 --rooms 4
Add-ons for fridge, oven, and windows:8 are included
A 10% biweekly recurring discount is applied
The total price falls within the $250-$500 deep clean range before add-ons
Estimated duration is provided

evals.prompt: Show me today's full schedule and tell me if Alpha Team can fit in one more standard clean in the NW zone around 2pm.
evals.expected_output: Display today's schedule for all crews, then check Alpha Team's availability — show their current jobs, total hours, and whether a 2-hour standard clean starting at 2pm fits without overlap. Recommend the slot if available.
expectations: The schedule-view command is run for today's date
The crew-status command is run to check Alpha Team availability
Alpha Team's current hours and remaining capacity are stated
A clear yes/no recommendation is given for the 2pm NW slot
If yes, the schedule-add command is shown or suggested

evals.prompt: We're coming up on May and August — our two biggest months because of UF. Run the forecast for April through September and tell me how many crews I need and what to do to prepare.
evals.expected_output: A 6-month seasonal forecast showing monthly multipliers, projected revenue, jobs/day, crews needed, and specific actionable recommendations for the May move-out surge and August move-in peak.
expectations: The forecast command is run with --start-month for April and --months 6
May shows a 1.40x multiplier with move-out surge language
August shows a 1.80x multiplier as peak demand
Crew hiring recommendations are included for May and August
SW zone is specifically mentioned as the UF-area hotspot
Summer slow months (June/July) are flagged for training/maintenance

evals.prompt: Look up client Sarah Johnson — show me her full profile, service history, and what she's scheduled for next. Then search for any other clients in the NW zone.
evals.expected_output: Full client profile for Sarah Johnson with contact info, address, preferences (eco-friendly, two dogs), service history, upcoming jobs, lifetime value. Then a filtered list of all NW zone clients.
expectations: The client-view command is used for Sarah Johnson's client ID
Her preferences (eco-friendly products, two dogs) are visible in the output
Service history with dates, types, prices, and ratings is shown
The client-list command is run with --zone NW filter
Multiple NW zone clients are listed with lifetime values

evals.prompt: Give me a commercial cleaning quote for a dental office that's 3500 sqft, and a standard apartment quote for a 2-bed 950 sqft unit near UF with the move-out add-on package (fridge, oven, carpet for 2 rooms).
evals.expected_output: Two separate quotes: (1) Commercial medical-rate quote for 3500 sqft dental office at $0.30/sqft = ~$1,050. (2) Standard clean for 2BR/950sqft apartment with fridge ($35), oven ($40), and carpet:2 ($50) add-ons.
expectations: Two separate quote commands are run
The dental office uses --commercial-type medical at $0.30/sqft rate
The commercial quote totals around $1,050
The apartment quote uses --service-type standard with correct sqft and rooms
Add-ons fridge, oven, and carpet:2 are applied to the apartment quote
Both quotes show itemized line items and totals
